frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: I had asked her for coffee twice and she kept saying she's not interested, but frankly these days many girls say that first out of attitude. Later I found out she is already with someone, so I immediately stepped back out of respect for that man and their relationship. If she had just said clearly she was committed, there would have been no issue at all.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because the speaker treats the woman's own refusal as negotiable but respects her unavailability only once another man's claim is known. It encodes the surface manifestation of ignoring direct disinterest and backing off only after learning she is 'taken,' framed as reasonable respect.
original_frame: Legal System Weaponisation Narrative: Women Who File DV and Dowry Cases Are Liars
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 45. Married. An upper caste Hindu man in Pune. You are posting under your real name on a men's rights Facebook group with 80000 members after the Supreme Court made a statement about misuse of Section 498A. You are writing a detailed comment about your own experience Ñ your brother was falsely accused, your parents were arrested, the case was eventually dropped but the damage was done. You are using your brother's case to make a broader argument about what Indian women do with dowry laws and what needs to change and what men and their families are living through while feminists celebrate these laws.
